LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, JOSE CISNEROS, TREASURE AND TAX COLLECTOR'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST, THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE DETERMINATION OF THE MERITS OF THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION REQUIRES FURTHER FACTUAL DEVELOPMENT AND CANNOT BE RESOLVED AT THE PLEADING STAGE. THE COMPLAINT AND JUDICIALLY NOTICEABLE MATERIALS DO NOT SHOW THAT PROPOSITION Q VIOLATES PROPOSITION 218, SECTION 17041.5 OR THE SINGLE SUBJECT RULE NOR HAS PLAINTIFF SHOWN THAT IT CAN ALLEGE SUFFICIENT FACTS TO STATE COGNIZABLE CLAIMS ON THESE MATTERS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

AMENDED DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-11-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. AMY L. SILVERSTEIN & ATTY. JOHANNA W. ROBERTS OF SILVERSTEIN & POMERANTZ LLP, TEL. NO. 593-3502 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. JAMES M. EMERY, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-4628 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, JOSE CISNEROS, TREASURE AND TAX COLLECTOR'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled as to the second cause of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the first, third and fourth causes of action. The determination of the merits of the second cause of action requires further factual development and cannot be resolved at the pleading stage. The complaint and judicially noticeable materials do not show that Proposition Q violates Proposition 218, section 17041.5 or the single subject rule nor has plaintiff shown that it can allege sufficient facts to state cognizable claims on these matters. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/HEK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-05-2012 Text Ruling
